subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the theme of resilience and strength of nature
Interpret the poet’s depiction of trees and human interaction with nature
Identify and understand literary devices such as imagery, metaphor, and repetition
Reflect on environmental and ethical implications of the poem
Develop critical thinking through discussion and analysis

lesson_plan.title: Introduction and Context
topics_covered: Background of the poet and poem
Significance of trees and nature in human life
Poet’s message about strength and perseverance
teaching_strategy: Teacher reads the poem aloud with clear expression
Explain difficult words and phrases contextually
student_activities: Note down new words and phrases with meanings

lesson_plan.title: Theme Analysis
topics_covered: Difficulty of killing a tree: physical and symbolic meaning
Tree as a symbol of life, resilience, and nature’s power
Human attempts to control or harm nature
teaching_strategy: Provide real-life examples of tree resilience and environmental importance
student_activities: Write stanza-wise main idea in notebook
Discuss examples of human impact on nature

lesson_plan.title: Poetic Devices
topics_covered: Imagery describing tree, roots, bark, and branches
Repetition to emphasize difficulty of destroying a tree
Metaphors relating tree to life and persistence
teaching_strategy: Highlight key examples of imagery, repetition, and metaphor
Explain effect on reader and theme
student_activities: Underline examples of literary devices in the poem
Explain their meaning and effect in own words

lesson_plan.title: Critical Interpretation
topics_covered: Symbolism of tree in human life and environment
Connection between poet’s message and environmental awareness
teaching_strategy: Discuss symbolic and ethical layers of the poem
Encourage debate on human responsibility towards nature
student_activities: Write a short paragraph interpreting symbolic meaning of the tree

lesson_plan.title: Moral and Application
topics_covered: Lesson on patience, respect for nature, and perseverance
teaching_strategy: Guide students to reflect on the poem’s moral
Encourage examples from daily life and local environment
student_activities: Write one paragraph on moral lesson learned
Give examples of trees and plants showing resilience or importance

teaching_aids: Pictures of trees, forests, and nature
teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyze the poet’s depiction of nature, understand symbolic meanings, and reflect on environmental and ethical lessons.
